rules.rule: Foundation services MUST wrap exactly one broker — no more, no less.
Foundation services MUST validate all inputs before calling brokers.
Foundation services MUST map broker exceptions to service-layer exceptions (ValidationException, DependencyException, ServiceException).
Service methods MUST use try-catch pattern when calling brokers.
Foundation services MUST use dependency injection to receive broker instances.
Service method names SHOULD mirror broker method names (e.g., broker: `getPatientAsync` → service: `retrievePatientByIdAsync`).
Services MUST implement TryCatch wrapper for exception mapping and logging.
Foundation services MUST NOT contain UI logic (formatting, display strings, className generation).
Foundation services MUST NOT call other services — composition happens at processing/orchestration layer.
Services MUST define clear exception types: ValidationException, DependencyException, ServiceException.
Services MUST validate for null/undefined inputs.
Services MUST validate required string fields are not empty.
Services MUST validate required ID fields exist before retrieval operations.
Services SHOULD validate structural integrity (e.g., valid email format, date ranges).
Validation exceptions MUST include the field name and reason for failure.
Validation MUST happen before broker calls, not after.
Broker exceptions MUST be caught and wrapped in DependencyException.
Validation failures MUST throw ValidationException with descriptive messages.
Unexpected service errors MUST be wrapped in ServiceException.
All service exceptions MUST preserve the inner exception for debugging.
Exception messages MUST be user-facing and actionable.
Foundation services MUST be in `services/foundations/{domain}/` directory.
Service class names MUST follow pattern `{Domain}Service` (e.g., `PatientService`).
Service interface names MUST follow pattern `I{Domain}Service` (e.g., `IPatientService`).
Services SHOULD expose interfaces for testing and dependency inversion.
Each service file MUST contain one service class only.
